projectScope.objective: palantir-math Sequencer JSXGraph canvas enhancement: real-time drag intersection tracking, absolute value fold visualization, and teaching annotation features — maximizing JSXGraph 1.12.2 native API usage per user directive
backendDomains: DATA: IntersectionPoint, CoordinateLabel, FunctiongraphScene, DragState, FoldGeometry, GraphFamilyProfile, TracePath
LOGIC: IntersectionSolver (IntersectionResolverInterface), AbsValueFoldComputer, DependencyGraph, DragBehaviorInterface, DerivativeEvaluator
ACTION: DragCascadeUpdate, BatchBoardUpdate, AnnotationToggle, TraceCurveRecord, AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate
SECURITY: InputSanitization (LaTeX → new Function() XSS boundary)
frontendSurfaces: EnhancedJSXGraphCanvas — main interactive canvas with real-time native cascade updates
IntersectionLabelOverlay — dynamic coordinate display on intersection points via JSXGraph Text elements
AbsValueFoldVisualization — four-layer fold: primary f(x)+ty, |f(x)+ty|, reflection guide (dashed), fold boundary line at y=0
TeacherDragDemo — instructor drags f(x) via parentPoint, all dependents update via native _dep chain
IntersectionDiscovery — drag two curves toward each other, watch intersection appear/vanish via otherintersection
AbsValueTeaching — live |f(x)| fold change demonstration as f(x) translates vertically

ddd: IntersectionPoint named per JSXGraph intersection domain vocabulary — matches JXG.Intersection element name exactly
CoordinateLabel maps to JXG.Text element — no invented naming layer
DragState.ty follows existing functiongraphDragSemantics.ts convention — no reinvention of delta semantics
IntersectionResolverInterface uses JSXGraph Numerics vocabulary (generalizedNewton, chandrupatla, root) for all solver variants
DragBehaviorInterface extends graphFamily.ts taxonomy (Linear/Quadratic/Cubic/Quartic/Abs) — consistent with existing classification vocabulary
AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate aligns with existing unwrapOuterAbsoluteExpression() naming in jsxGraphRenderer.ts
dry: board.reducedUpdate=true eliminates all manual element.update() calls in drag handlers — single source of cascade truth
Single IntersectionPoint entity per intersection index — not duplicated per drag frame, updated in-place
CoordinateLabel format (toFixed(2)) declared once in contentFn closure — not repeated per render
Single ty value per function in DragState Struct — aggregated by DragStateReducer, not copied per observer
LaTeX→JS function LRU cache (200-entry, existing in latexToJSXGraph.ts) prevents recompilation — no secondary cache layer
board.suspendUpdate()/unsuspendUpdate() wrapping declares single batch repaint boundary — not per-element
ocp: IntersectionPoint structure is fixed — solver implementation (NativeIntersection vs NewtonRaphson) plugs into IntersectionResolverInterface without modifying entity
CoordinateLabel format extensible (add slope, degrees, units) via contentFn update — no lifecycle modification required
FunctiongraphScene open to new element types (Derivative, OtherIntersection, TracePath) without modifying scene identity or serialization contract
DragState Struct extensible (add tx for horizontal drag) without modifying DragStateReducer or IntersectionSolver
DragBehaviorInterface open for new graph families (Piecewise, Parametric) without modifying Linear/Quadratic/Abs handlers — OCP confirmed by PR #90 (AbsDragBehavior added without Linear regression)
pecs: IntersectionPoint: Consumer of two JXG.Curve parents (super/extends — accepts any functiongraph pair), Producer of {x, y} coordinate (specific/extends — typed IntersectionCoordinate)
CoordinateLabel: Consumer of IntersectionPoint position (super — accepts any {X(), Y()} point), Producer of formatted string (extends — specific '(x.xx, y.yy)' format)
DragState: Producer of ty/tx delta (extends — specific to drag context), consumed by IntersectionSolver and AbsValueFoldComputer (super — accept any numeric delta)
IntersectionResolverInterface: Consumer of function pair (super — any (f, g) returning number), Producer of {x, y} intersection (extends — specific resolved coordinate)
DragBehaviorInterface: Consumer of DragState (super — any delta struct), Producer of element mutation (extends — specific per graph family)

primitives: ObjectType — IntersectionPoint (identity: intersection index + parent pair), CoordinateLabel (identity: which intersection it labels), FunctiongraphScene (identity: board ID), GraphFamilyProfile (identity: functiongraph element ID)
Struct (Value Type) — DragState { ty: number, tx?: number }, FoldGeometry { ty: number, foldBoundaryXValues: number[], reflectionGuidePoints: [number, number][], absMaxY: number }

Interface — IntersectionResolverInterface (polymorphic solver: NativeIntersection | NewtonRaphsonSolver), DragBehaviorInterface (per family: LinearDragBehavior | QuadraticDragBehavior | AbsDragBehavior | PiecewiseDragBehavior)
Reducer — DragStateReducer (collapse N drag-frame ty updates → canonical DragState), TracePathReducer (push/shift bounded 300-entry circular buffer)

Derived Property — DerivativeAt(curve, x): slope derived from functiongraph via board.create('derivative', [curve]) at intersection x; IntersectionCoordinate: derived from two functiongraph closures sharing parentPoint
ObjectBackedLinkType — IntersectionPointLink (connects two FunctiongraphScene elements at resolved coordinate, carries index and solver variant metadata)

data.name: IntersectionPoint
data.description: Resolved (x, y) coordinate pair where two functiongraphs intersect on the JSXGraph canvas. Created via board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0]) (H-A native) or JXG.Math.Numerics.root() output (fallback). Has identity: intersection index + parent pair.

data.name: CoordinateLabel
data.description: Dynamic text element displaying (x, y) of an IntersectionPoint in real time. board.create('text', [xFn, yFn, contentFn]) — all three args are function closures auto-evaluated on every board update. Has identity: which intersection it labels.

data.name: FunctiongraphScene
data.description: JSXGraph board scene document: all declared elements (functiongraphs, points, sliders, text, auxiliary curves), configuration params, and auto-intersection metadata. Persisted in JSXGraphScene type in seq-data.ts. Identity: board ID.

data.name: DragState
data.description: Struct value type: current translation vector { ty: number, tx?: number } applied to a dragged functiongraph. No identity — just the current delta value at a given drag frame. PR #90 confirmed ty semantics in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts.

data.name: FoldGeometry
data.description: Struct: geometric description of |f(x)+ty| fold visualization. { ty: number, foldBoundaryXValues: number[], reflectionGuidePoints: [number, number][], absMaxY: number }. Describes fold appearance at given ty — independent of computation. No native JSXGraph equivalent.

data.name: GraphFamilyProfile
data.description: Classification result per functiongraph: { family: 'Linear'|'Quadratic'|'Cubic'|'Quartic'|'Abs'|'Piecewise'|'Parametric', isOuterAbs: boolean, dragModel: DragBehaviorKey }. Extended by PR #90 with outer-absolute recognition in graphFamily.ts.

data.name: TracePath
data.description: Bounded circular buffer (max 300 entries) of (x, y) positions recorded during functiongraph drag for teaching playback. Custom implementation required — JSXGraph native Tracecurve requires Glider mover, not applicable to free functiongraph drag (eval-suite-A-001 confirmed, ev-009).

logic.name: IntersectionSolver
logic.description: IntersectionResolverInterface: polymorphic real-time root-finding for f(x)=g(x) during drag. H-A primary: NativeIntersection (board.create('intersection') with v1.12.1 improved curve-curve precision). Fallback: NewtonRaphsonSolver (JXG.Math.Numerics.root with warm-start lastX). Interface decouples strategy from IntersectionPoint entity.

logic.name: AbsValueFoldComputer
logic.description: Computes FoldGeometry from |f(x)+ty|: finds x-intercepts (foldBoundaryXValues via JXG.Math.Numerics.root), generates reflectionGuidePoints, computes absMaxY. Custom code required — JSXGraph has no native abs-fold element. Output feeds AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate.

logic.name: DragBehaviorInterface
logic.description: Polymorphic drag behavior per graph family. Implementations: LinearDragBehavior, QuadraticDragBehavior, AbsDragBehavior (translate-source-before-outer-abs — implemented in PR #90), PiecewiseDragBehavior (planned Phase 3). OCP: new families open without modifying existing handlers.

logic.name: DragSemanticResolver
logic.description: Maps GraphFamilyProfile → DragBehaviorInterface implementation. Resolves which drag model to apply per curve type. Extends graphFamily.ts classification. PR #90 confirmed: outer-abs detection routes to AbsDragBehavior without Linear regression.

logic.name: DerivativeEvaluator
logic.description: Slope/derivative at intersection and critical points. H-A: board.create('derivative', [curve]) — unused native element, validated in proto-jxg-A-001 (claim-001: plugs into fg1._dep chain, no custom slope computation). Derived property from functiongraph.

action.name: DragCascadeUpdate
action.description: Triggers dependent element recomputation on drag event. H-A: parentPoint.setPosition() → JSXGraph native _dep chain auto-cascades all dependents (IntersectionPoint, CoordinateLabel, DerivativeEvaluator) without manual .update() calls. board.reducedUpdate=true scopes cascade to drag subtree only.

action.name: BatchBoardUpdate
action.description: board.suspendUpdate() → mutate N elements → board.unsuspendUpdate() → single DOM repaint. Eliminates N intermediate redraws during element construction or multi-element mutation. Validated in proto-jxg-A-001 wrapping all 10 element declarations (ev-004).

action.name: AnnotationToggle
action.description: Activates/deactivates teaching overlay elements (CoordinateLabel, DerivativeEvaluator curve, TracePath display) per session mode. §ORCH-10 compliance: all annotation elements declared explicitly as board elements with lifecycle — no ad-hoc DOM mutations.

action.name: TraceCurveRecord
action.description: Captures drag path for teaching playback. Custom implementation: push current element position to TracePath.dataX/dataY in board.on('update') callback, then call traceElement.update(). Native Tracecurve not applicable — requires Glider mover per JSXGraph docs (ev-009 confirmed by eval-suite-A-001).

action.name: AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate
action.description: Updates |f(x)+ty| four-layer visualization: (1) primary f(x)+ty curve, (2) |f(x)+ty| abs curve via Math.abs() wrapper sharing ty closure, (3) dashed reflection guide y=-f_raw(x) for negative fold region, (4) fold boundary line at y=0 between x-intercepts. PR #90 implemented drag semantics; fold line layers 3-4 remain for Phase 2.

security.name: InputSanitization
security.description: LaTeX → JS function compilation XSS boundary enforced at new Function() call site in latexToJSXGraph.ts. Existing 200-entry LRU cache must not be bypassed. No additional new Function() call sites permitted. All new drag-handler function closures must reference pre-compiled functions only.

learn.name: InteractionTelemetry
learn.description: Records drag events, intersection discoveries, and frame timing per session. Captures: frameCount, intersection event timestamps, solver iteration counts, annotation usage frequency. Feeds TeachingEffectiveness evaluation. Declared as typed LEARN ref (feedbackEntityRef) in Phase 3.

learn.name: TeachingEffectiveness
learn.description: Evaluation artifact: annotation usage rates, dwell time on intersection labels, drag interaction patterns. Feeds refinement of CoordinateLabel position, FoldGeometry clarity, derivative display configuration. Evaluation criteria to be formalized in Phase 3.

recommendedStack.name: JSXGraph Native-First Canvas Enhancement Stack (H-A)

technologies.name: JSXGraph 1.12.2
strengths: Native intersection element with v1.12.1 improved precision for curve-curve (claim-009)
board.reducedUpdate=true for 40-60% frame computation reduction during drag (claim-005)
Native Derivative element: board.create('derivative', [curve]) — validated in proto-jxg-A-001 (claim-001)
Dynamic text auto-updates via function closures in board.create('text', [xFn, yFn, contentFn]) (claim-016, claim-017)
board.on('update') fires every drag frame for telemetry without polling (claim-013)
otherintersection support for multiple intersection points with forbidden-point arrays (claim-011)
JXG.Math.Numerics.root() and generalizedNewton() available as fallback solver (claim-004, claim-027)
weaknesses: Tracecurve requires Glider mover — cannot track free functiongraph drag path natively (claim-003, ev-009)
No native absolute value fold visualization element — custom AbsValueFoldComputer required
parentPoint migration required to move existing raw DOM drag handlers to native _dep chain (Phase 2 deliverable)

technologies.name: TypeScript + Bun
strengths: Type-safe JSXGraph element creation with project-level jsxgraph.d.ts
bun test runner for eval suites — proto-jxg-A-001 and proto-jxg-B-001 both achieved tscErrors=0
Zero external runtime dependencies for core schemas
weaknesses: JSXGraph type definitions (jsxgraph.d.ts) require project-level maintenance as JSXGraph API evolves

recommendedStack.rationale: JSXGraph 1.12.2 native APIs cover ~90% of requirements without custom code: native intersection auto-updates eliminate solver wiring for the common case; board.reducedUpdate=true provides 60fps performance budget for 10+ elements; native Derivative element provides slope display at zero additional code cost. Custom code is bounded to: (1) TracePath buffer (Tracecurve Glider limitation), (2) |f(x)| fold visualization (no native abs-fold), (3) piecewise/parametric drag semantics. Aligns precisely with user directive (native-first mandate at 2026-04-11T14:45:00Z) and achieves evaluator ACCEPT verdict (confidence 0.88).

alternatives.name: H-B Custom Interaction Layer (hyp-jxg-B)
alternatives.whyNot: Confirmed architectural race condition FM-B-001 (dragState.ty stale read between DOM addEventListener and board.on('update') — HIGH severity, N-1 stale frames under rapid drag burst, eval-suite-B-001 B-T9). Permanent performance ceiling: board.reducedUpdate=true incompatible with H-B observer pattern. DDD violation (mixed vocabulary), OCP violation (DependencyGraph as implicit hub), DRY risk (duplicate cascade management). Valid fallback ONLY if: JSXGraph native intersection fails at abs-fold kinks in browser testing, OR element count permanently stays ≤5, OR JSXGraph drops _dep chain support.

forwardProp.description: Ontology contracts flow forward through seq-data.ts type declarations into jsxGraphRenderer.ts board element creation, then into JSXGraphCanvas.tsx live SVG updates via JSXGraph native _dep cascade
steps: IntersectionPoint (ontology) → IntersectionCoordinate Struct contract in seq-data.ts
IntersectionCoordinate contract → board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0]) in jsxGraphRenderer.ts
FunctiongraphScene (ontology) → JSXGraphScene type in seq-data.ts → board initialization in JSXGraphCanvas.tsx
DragBehaviorInterface (ontology) → DragSemanticResolver routing in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts → AbsDragBehavior / LinearDragBehavior dispatch
FoldGeometry (ontology) → FoldGeometry struct contract in seq-data.ts → AbsValueFoldComputer output → AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate four-layer curve construction in jsxGraphRenderer.ts
CoordinateLabel (ontology) → TextElementContract {xFn, yFn, contentFn} → board.create('text') in jsxGraphRenderer.ts
board.reducedUpdate=true → native _dep chain cascade → IntersectionPoint + CoordinateLabel + DerivativeEvaluator auto-update on every drag frame without manual wiring
GraphFamilyProfile (ontology) → graphFamily.ts classification output → DragSemanticResolver → DragBehaviorInterface dispatch
gaps: FoldGeometry → fold line display (layers 3-4: reflection guide + fold boundary at y=0) not yet implemented — PR #90 only covers drag semantics level
CoordinateLabel → TextElementContract not yet a formally declared type in seq-data.ts (relies on inline board.create args)
InteractionTelemetry → no declared telemetry hook in JSXGraphCanvas.tsx yet (Phase 3 deliverable)
parentPoint migration not yet applied to all existing raw DOM addEventListener drag handlers in jsxGraphRenderer.ts (Phase 2 deliverable)
DerivativeEvaluator → board.create('derivative', [curve]) not yet wired into jsxGraphRenderer.ts scene construction (Phase 2 deliverable)

backwardProp.description: Runtime canvas events flow backward into InteractionTelemetry lineage, TeachingEffectiveness evaluation, and ontology refinement for solver warm-start and overlay design
steps: Drag frame events (board.on('update')) → InteractionTelemetry.dragFrames push (lineage capture)
InteractionTelemetry.dragFrames → frame timing, solver iteration count → performance evaluation
Intersection discovery events (IntersectionPoint visibility change) → event log in InteractionTelemetry (lineage)
IntersectionPoint coordinate accuracy → IntersectionResolverInterface warm-start strategy refinement (LEARN)
TeachingEffectiveness metrics (annotation dwell, label usage rates) → CoordinateLabel position and FoldGeometry clarity refinement
Solver convergence failures / edge cases (asymptotes, tangent intersections) → root-finder parameter tuning → IntersectionSolver evaluation update
FM-A-001 (test harness defect at fold kink) → exp-jxg-01 browser validation → native intersection precision verdict → IntersectionResolverInterface strategy update if kink fails
gaps: InteractionTelemetry not yet declared as typed LEARN ref (feedbackEntityRef) in seq-data.ts — heuristic only at present
TeachingEffectiveness evaluation criteria not formally defined — qualitative annotation usage observation only
exp-jxg-01 (fold boundary precision at abs-fold kink) deferred to browser testing — verdict not yet closed
No evaluationEntityRef declared for IntersectionSolver accuracy — informal precision notes in eval-results.json only
No refinementSignalEntityRef for DragBehaviorInterface parameter tuning from telemetry data

implementationStrategy.name: Golden Tables
implementationStrategy.description: Establish stable data shape contracts in seq-data.ts before adding live logic. Finalize IntersectionPoint, CoordinateLabel, DragState, FoldGeometry, GraphFamilyProfile, TracePath type declarations. These DATA layer facts anchor all subsequent logic and action layers.
deliverables: IntersectionPoint and CoordinateLabel type contracts in seq-data.ts (IntersectionCoordinate struct, TextElementContract interface)
FoldGeometry struct type: { ty: number, foldBoundaryXValues: number[], reflectionGuidePoints: [number, number][], absMaxY: number }
GraphFamilyProfile extended with PiecewiseFamily and ParametricFamily variants in seq-data.ts and graphFamily.ts
TracePath type: circular buffer struct with dataX: number[], dataY: number[], maxSize: 300
IntersectionResolverInterface: { solve(f: (x:number)=>number, g: (x:number)=>number, warmStart?: number): IntersectionCoordinate | null }
DragBehaviorInterface: { apply(element: JXG.Curve, dragState: DragState): void }

implementationStrategy.name: Operational Decision-Making
implementationStrategy.description: Add real-time logic and interactive actions on top of Phase 1 contracts. Migrate functiongraph drag to parentPoint model for native _dep cascade. Wire native intersection, coordinate labels, derivative display, batch updates, and abs-fold visualization layers into jsxGraphRenderer.ts.
deliverables: parentPoint migration in jsxGraphRenderer.ts: replace raw DOM addEventListener with parentPoint.setPosition() drag model for all functiongraphs
board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0/1]) for all autoIntersection pairs: replaces static visibility state machine with live auto-updating native elements
board.create('otherintersection', [fg1, fg2, i1]) for second intersection points with forbidden-point exclusion (v1.9.0+)
board.create('text', [xFn, yFn, contentFn]) dynamic CoordinateLabels on all IntersectionPoints
board.create('derivative', [curve]) for derivative/slope display at intersection (new native element — validated in proto-jxg-A-001)
AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate: four-layer abs curve construction in jsxGraphRenderer.ts (primary + abs + reflection guide + fold boundary)
board.reducedUpdate=true set immediately after board initialization in JSXGraphCanvas.tsx
TracePath: board.on('update') push to dataX/dataY arrays + curve.update() for drag history recording
BatchBoardUpdate (suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate) wrapping all element construction phases
dependencies: Phase 1 type contracts declared in seq-data.ts
JSXGraph 1.12.2 native APIs: board.create('intersection'), board.create('derivative'), board.create('text'), board.create('otherintersection')
exp-jxg-01 browser validation (can proceed in parallel — fallback to JXG.Math.Numerics.root() if kink precision fails)

implementationStrategy.name: AI-First
implementationStrategy.description: Declare InteractionTelemetry as typed LEARN ref. Formalize TeachingEffectiveness evaluation. Add piecewise/parametric drag semantics. Close exp-jxg-01 verdict. Establish refinement loop from telemetry to solver and overlay parameter tuning.
deliverables: InteractionTelemetry typed ref declaration in seq-data.ts (feedbackEntityRef, workflowLineageEntityRef)
TeachingEffectiveness evaluation criteria formalized: annotation dwell thresholds, intersection discovery rate, drag interaction patterns
DragBehaviorInterface for Piecewise family: segment-aware drag constraint per piecewise interval
DragBehaviorInterface for Parametric family: separate x(t)/y(t) drag model
AnnotationToggle: explicit element lifecycle management for all teaching overlay elements per §ORCH-10
exp-jxg-01 closure: browser test of native intersection precision at abs-fold kink — produce final IntersectionResolverInterface strategy verdict
Refinement loop: solver warm-start parameter tuning from InteractionTelemetry solver iteration counts
evaluationEntityRef and refinementSignalEntityRef declared for IntersectionSolver accuracy tracking
dependencies: InteractionTelemetry data from Phase 2 runtime (minimum 1 teaching session)
Browser testing environment for exp-jxg-01 (JSXGraph-in-browser — tsc-only validation insufficient)

evaluatorReason: H-A (JSXGraph native-first) accepted. All 15 R-gates PASS. R1: all 14 sources tier-1/2. R2: no unresolved contradictions blocking H-A. R3: 8 scenarios linked (4 per hypothesis). R4: 3 failure modes documented (FM-A-001, FM-B-001, FM-B-002). R5: all evidence current (JSXGraph 1.12.2 is current release). R6: all sources tagged [Official]/[Synthesis]/[Inference] with confidence scores. R7: win rationale produced (rec-jxg-001). R8: H-B documented as competing alternative. R9: all scenarios sufficient or partial — none insufficient. R10: 4 reversal conditions documented. R11: 20 objects across 5 D/L/A/S/LEARN domains. R12: DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS applied to all objects; H-B violations flagged. R13: H-A ForwardProp HEALTHY (native cascade); BackwardProp partial (telemetry not yet declared). R14: both prototypes buildStatus=success, tscErrors=0. R15: H-A passRate=1.0 (30/30), H-B passRate=1.0 (29/29). No debate triggered: pass rate difference=0%, no D/L/A misclassification, FM-B-001 predicted by sc-jxg-B-adv. Confidence: 0.88. H-B disqualified by FM-B-001 HIGH-severity architectural race condition (dragState.ty stale read — N-1 stale frames under rapid drag) and permanent board.reducedUpdate=true performance ceiling.
